name: Ibrahim ibn Ali
title: Relic of the Lion of God
shortDescription: Another son of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) who fought alongside his brother Imam Hussain (AS) and was martyred in Karbala.
fullStory:
Ibrahim ibn Ali (AS) is one of the brave sons of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS) whose presence and martyrdom in Karbala are recorded in some Maqatil and genealogical sources.

The extensive presence of Amir al-Mu'minin's (AS) sons in Karbala demonstrates their deep insight and loyalty to the Imam of their time. Ibrahim, like his other brothers (Abbas, Uthman, Abu Bakr, etc.), embarked on this one-way journey to assist the Master of Martyrs.

On the day of Ashura, he entered the battlefield with firm faith and resolve. With the name and memory of his father Ali (AS) on his lips, Ibrahim charged into the heart of the enemy army, dealing heavy blows to them.

Finally, after a manifest battle and taking down a number of Kufans, he was surrounded by Yazid's mercenaries and was martyred by spear and sword strikes, his soul flying to the heavens.
quoteArabic: وَمِنْ وُلْدِ عَلِيٍّ أَيْضاً: إِبْراهِيمُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ...
quote: (In genealogical books:) And from the sons of Ali (AS) also: Ibrahim ibn Ali was killed in Karbala.
